incorporations.reason: Italian 'tante amarezze' derives from 'amaro' (bitter); 'bitterness' is the literal and more precise rendering, whereas 'afflictions' broadens the sense away from the original metaphor

The Castello Sforzesco is a specific Milanese landmark; retaining the Italian proper noun preserves the cultural-historical referent, consistent with keeping other Italian place names (Piazza Mercanti, Belfiore) untranslated
Same rationale as paragraph 15 — consistency in retaining the Castello as a proper noun throughout the chapter
